frameworks.name: Bayesian canonical correlation analysis
frameworks.one_line: latent-variable model linking image pixels to fMRI voxels for modular encoding/decoding

frameworks.name: modular encoding/decoding of fMRI
frameworks.one_line: decomposes complex stimuli into elements via data-driven modules instead of hand-crafted bases

frameworks.name: multiscale local image bases
frameworks.one_line: prior approach used heuristically determined image-basis shapes for reconstruction

core_question: Can encoding and decoding modules between visual stimuli and fMRI activity be extracted automatically from data instead of being hand-designed?
key_assumptions: stimuli and brain activity share latent variables relating pixel sets to voxel sets
complex stimuli can be represented as combinations of simple elements
linear latent mappings suffice for stimulus-brain correspondence
manipulations: automatic extraction of modules from data versus heuristic image bases
use of estimated mappings to build both encoding and decoding models
key_findings: the model recovers a modular representation with spatially localized multiscale image bases
estimated latent-to-pixel mappings function as data-driven image bases
resulting encoding and decoding models produce accurate predictions of brain activity and stimulus images
interpretations: Bayesian CCA can automatically learn stimulus-brain modular mappings comparable to hand-crafted bases
shared latent variables provide a unified basis for both encoding and decoding
